rationale: Hard pass for Helix Jump. The archetype's muted earth-tone palette (navy, brown, olive) and detailed 2D illustrated cross-section style are the polar opposite of the game's neon, 3D minimalist geometric look — there is no visual bridge a publishing team could exploit without rebuilding the creative from scratch. While a vertical "descending through layers" cross-section concept could theoretically be reskinned to evoke the helix tower's downward fall (the one mechanic hook worth testing), the curiosity/edutainment audience skews older and more passive than Helix Jump's reflex-driven hypercasual players, and the single-creative/single-advertiser signal offers weak validation. Don't prioritize; if tested at all, only as a stylized "fall through layers" remix using the game's neon palette.
